brand.name: DNS Realty Group
brand.vertical: Local & service — residential real estate brokerage

offer.what: Full-service residential real estate brokerage — buyer representation, home listings/seller representation, free home valuations, relocation services, and short sale/foreclosure expertise across the DC metro area (Maryland, Virginia, DC)
offer.coreOutcome: Buying or selling a home confidently at the best possible price, with expert local guidance and a stress-free transaction from first contact to closing
offer.pricePoint: Commission-based (no upfront cost to buyers; seller commission typically 5–6% split at closing); free home valuation and seller CMA available as entry offers
offer.commitment: Transaction-based (single deal per engagement); relationship-oriented for repeat and referral business over a 3–7 year homeownership cycle
leadMagnets: Free home valuation — 'What's My Home Worth?' tool (address entry)
Free comparative market analysis (CMA) for prospective sellers on request
Educational blog covering buying, selling, investing, and financing

audience.icp: First-time and move-up homebuyers and residential sellers in Southern Maryland and the greater DC metro area (MD, VA, DC); skews toward millennials, families, and relocating professionals
painPoints: Fear of overpaying (buyers) or underpricing (sellers) in a fast-moving market
Overwhelm navigating mortgages, inspections, contingencies, and closing without guidance
Uncertainty about market timing and neighborhood values
Difficulty finding a trustworthy agent with deep local/community knowledge
Confusion about down-payment assistance programs, short sales, and foreclosure processes
segments: First-time homebuyers needing education, hand-holding, and financing navigation
Move-up or downsizing sellers needing accurate pricing strategy and listing exposure
Relocating professionals moving into the DC metro region
Residential real estate investors seeking buy-and-hold or value-add properties
Millennials entering homeownership for the first time

salesCycle.length: Weeks to months — active property search typically 30–90+ days; transaction close 30–60 days after contract
salesCycle.trial: Yes — free home valuation for sellers; no-cost initial buyer consultation; no obligation to sign until ready
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Agent consultation → signed buyer or listing agreement → full transaction management through closing
salesCycle.topObjection: Belief they can search independently on Zillow/Realtor.com without an agent, or reluctance to pay commission — especially post-NAR settlement news

ahaMoment: Sellers: receiving a data-backed CMA that reveals their home's true market value and a clear pricing strategy. Buyers: being matched with a shortlist of properties that fit their real needs plus learning about down-payment assistance programs they didn't know existed.

retention.recurring: Low direct recurring revenue per client — revenue is transaction-based. Long-term retention value comes from repeat purchases (move-up, downsize, investment) and word-of-mouth referrals across a multi-year homeownership cycle.
retention.expansion: Referrals to friends/family, investment property transactions, commercial inquiries, relocation introductions, repeated transactions as life circumstances change
retention.churnRisk: One-and-done clients who close and never refer; warm leads who go cold during a long search and quietly switch to a competing agent

lifecycleGaps: No visible welcome or onboarding email sequence for new site registrants or lead form submissions
No lead-magnet nurture drip for home valuation tool users or property-search sign-ups
No consultation-conversion sequence to move warm/cold leads to a booked agent call
No post-close relationship sequence to generate referrals and stay top-of-mind
No value newsletter to maintain visibility during the long homeownership cycle between transactions
No win-back or re-engagement sequence for dormant leads who went cold
toneVoice: Approachable and trustworthy with a professional demeanor; locally rooted, educational, and community-focused. Conversational but credible — positioned to demystify real estate for everyday buyers and sellers, not to impress with industry jargon.
compliance: CAN-SPAM compliant required; Maryland real estate license disclosures (MD License #603140); Fair Housing Act language mandatory in all property-related communications; standard real estate advertising rules apply (no discriminatory language, accurate representations).
